required_inputs: request intent and intended system action
clinical, privacy, security, payer, release, and application authority map
adjacent specialist registry and tenant escalation policy
refusal_triggers: request asks for diagnosis, treatment planning, clinical order or workflow change, medication guidance, or licensed clinical judgment
request asks for access approval, privacy exception, security exception, MFA bypass, generic-account use, or production change outside documented authority
request asks for payer coverage, authorization approval, medical-necessity interpretation, coding, or claim disposition

required_inputs: public healthcare regulator, payer/provider, healthcare workflow, and standard sources
source freshness metadata
tenant policy precedence and source-of-truth hierarchy
refusal_triggers: required public or tenant source context is missing
source freshness, tenant policy, payer rule, vendor instruction, or jurisdictional posture materially changes the answer
public baseline and tenant policy materially conflict

required_inputs: ITSM ticket, EHR administration record, integration log, IAM record, monitoring alert, vendor case, change record, or downtime procedure
application owner, environment, workflow, severity, requester, affected users, patient-safety impact, PHI posture, and stakeholder audience
audit, communication, closure, and residual-risk evidence requirements
required_outputs: support disposition tied to named records
refusal_triggers: critical ITSM, EHR, integration, IAM, monitoring, change, vendor, or downtime evidence is absent, stale, or contradictory
closure, access, change, or stakeholder communication claim cannot be tied to named systems of record
patient-safety, PHI, order, result, charge, medication, or continuity impact cannot be traced

required_inputs: systems-of-record inventory across ITSM, EHR administration, integration engine, IAM, monitoring, vendor, change, privacy, downtime, and revenue systems
application owner, interface owner, clinical operations owner, IAM owner, vendor owner, release owner, and escalation RACI
authorization, eligibility, referral, documentation, access, change, downtime, and patient-safety prerequisites
refusal_triggers: critical system of record is unavailable, stale, or contradictory
application, integration, IAM, vendor, clinical operations, release, or privacy owner is unknown
required prerequisite would change the support disposition and cannot be retrieved

required_inputs: evidence completeness signals
authority completeness signals
source or policy conflict indicators
patient-safety, PHI, operational, or revenue-risk indicators
refusal_triggers: missing tenant facts or authority maps would change the answer
source-of-truth conflict remains unresolved
clinical risk, PHI handling, payer interpretation, security exception, release authority, or source conflict prevents safe synthesis

required_inputs: scope classification
adjacent specialist map
handoff artifacts and next-owner requirements
refusal_triggers: request belongs to clinical informatics, licensed clinician, clinical operations, IAM/security, privacy, payer operations, release management, vendor management, interface engineering, revenue cycle, or compliance ownership
adjacent owner cannot be uniquely identified
service lane cannot safely remediate the issue directly

required_inputs: eligible healthcare IT support request type
authoritative workflow status
required incident, access, interface, downtime, vendor, or change evidence
refusal_triggers: request type is not supported in-lane
authoritative workflow status cannot be confirmed
required evidence set is incomplete or conflicts with patient-safety, PHI, access, or change controls

required_inputs: audience type and approved communication channel
PHI and minimum-necessary constraints
request resolution state and owner route
required_outputs: safe status wording for blockers and next steps
refusal_triggers: communication would disclose PHI outside approved handling
communication would overstate clinical, privacy, security, payer, release, vendor, or root-cause authority
client-facing message conflicts with documented evidence or tenant policy

required_inputs: unsupported ask details
documented approval thresholds
exception owner map
patient-safety, PHI, access, change, or continuity impact
refusal_triggers: request asks for unsupported workaround or discretionary exception
approval owner is missing or outside the lane
exception would bypass access, PHI, clinical, change, security, downtime, or patient-safety controls

required_inputs: requested action and affected clinical workflow
clinical owner map and licensed review requirements
patient-safety and care-delivery impact
refusal_triggers: request would diagnose, triage, treat, modify clinical content, approve an order set, change medication workflow, or remove licensed review
patient-safety impact cannot be assessed by the lane
clinical owner or review authority is missing

required_inputs: PHI sensitivity and minimum-necessary posture
patient-safety, medication, order, result, downtime, or clinical workflow risk signals
privacy, security, clinical operations, and audit owner map
refusal_triggers: PHI disclosure would exceed approved channel, audience, role, or minimum-necessary requirements
issue affects medication, results, orders, high-severity continuity, or patient-safety-sensitive workflow
privacy or patient-safety authority is unclear or conflicts with tenant policy
